title: Mild Rain Brings Relief, Yellow Alert Issued For Vidarbha - The Times of India
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Maharashtra
full_text:
Nagpur: Mild rain and gusty winds brought temporary respite from the intense summer heat across Nagpur and other parts of Vidarbha on Saturday. Issuing a yellow alert for several districts of Vidarbha, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast more rain and thunderstorm activity on Sunday.
The sudden change in weather and accompanying winds led to at least five incidents of tree uprooting across Nagpur, briefly disrupting vehicular movement in different areas. According to fire department officials, trees were uprooted near Bhavan's School in Civil Lines, Power Grid Chowk, Jadhav Chowk near Cotton Market Fire Station, Date Layout in Trimurti Nagar and behind Gajanan Temple in the same locality.
The gusty winds and scattered showers were reported in different parts of Vidarbha. According to IMD data, Yavatmal recorded the highest rainfall at 12.3mm, followed by Washim at 7.6mm and Buldana at 7mm. Gondia recorded 3.6mm while Akola received 2.3mm of rainfall. Chandrapur and Gadchiroli too witnessed light rain activity.
Despite the rain, temperatures continued to hover around the 40 degrees Celsius mark in many districts.
Akola was the hottest place in Vidarbha at 42.2 degrees Celsius, while Wardha recorded 41.5 degrees. Nagpur, Amravati, and Buldhana registered maximum temperatures of 40.4 degrees Celsius.
The IMD has predicted that moisture incursion and local weather systems are likely to trigger more thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds over Vidarbha on Sunday. The yellow alert advises residents to remain cautious during sudden weather activity.
The sudden spell of rain, cloudy skies and strong winds, however, brought noticeable relief to residents after days of scorching heat conditions across the region.

title: IMD issues orange alert for Vidarbha; heavy rain likely across Maharashtra - The Live Nagpur
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Maharashtra
full_text:
A major change in weather conditions has brought relief from the scorching summer heat in Maharashtra, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing rain alerts for several parts of the state over the next 48 hours.
According to weather officials, atmospheric instability caused by a cyclonic system over North India is likely to trigger thunderstorms, gusty winds and moderate to heavy rainfall across many districts in Maharashtra.
Orange alert for VidarbhaThe impact is expected to be most severe in the Vidarbha region, where the IMD has issued an Orange Alert for Yavatmal and Chandrapur districts, warning of heavy rainfall and thunderstorm activity.A Yellow Alert has also been sounded for Akola, Amravati, Bhandara, Gondia, Nagpur and Washim districts.
Rain forecast for western Maharashtra, MarathwadaRain activity is also likely in parts of western Maharashtra and the Marathwada region. Districts including Kolhapur, Sangli and Solapur may experience thunderstorms accompanied by rain showers.
In Marathwada, rainfall has been predicted in Nanded, Latur and Dharashiv districts.Humid weather to continue in Mumbai, KonkanDespite the rainfall forecast in several regions, humid and warm conditions are expected to persist in Mumbai and parts of North Konkan, officials said.
Authorities have advised citizens, especially those in Vidarbha, to remain alert during thunderstorms and avoid unnecessary travel during heavy rain and lightning activity.

title: Heatwave preparedness directed by Union Health Secretary in India - The News Mill
full_text:
The Union Health Secretary has urged all State and Union Territory Chief Secretaries to strengthen their preparedness for the anticipated heatwave conditions forecast by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) from April to June 2026.
In a letter dated April 28, the Union Health Secretary advised operationalising dedicated Heat Stroke Management Units at all health facilities, ensuring sufficient ambulance services, disseminating early warnings for prompt action, and enabling real-time reporting of heat stroke cases through the Ministry’s Integrated Health Information Platform (IHIP) portal.
The IMD’s forecast indicates above-normal heatwave days across numerous regions, including East, Central, and North-West India, as well as the South-East Peninsula. Coastal areas of Odisha, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, and Andhra Pradesh are expected to be notably affected. Additionally, isolated regions of Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Karnataka may experience similar conditions.
Earlier, on April 26 and 27, the IMD reported maximum temperatures ranging from 40 to 46 degrees Celsius across most parts of the country, with exceptions such as the western Himalayan region, northeast Bihar, and northeast India, where temperatures remained below 36 degrees Celsius. The highest maximum temperature of 46.9 degrees Celsius was recorded in Akola.
The IMD forecast heatwave to severe heatwave conditions in isolated pockets of Himachal Pradesh on April 26 and heatwave conditions on April 27. Heatwave conditions are also expected in isolated areas of Jammu and Kashmir, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Bihar, Gujarat, and parts of Chhattisgarh between April 26 and 28.
Hot and humid weather is anticipated in isolated pockets of Tripura, coastal Maharashtra, and coastal Gujarat on April 26, with similar conditions forecast over Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, coastal Andhra Pradesh, Rayalaseema, and Kerala during the same period.
